text: The surgeon performs extensive circumferential dissection to free pelvic adhesions around the neorectum. The neorectum is fully mobilized and reduced into the abdominal cavity without tension. A tuovicral stitch is placed in the serosa of the neorectum and sutured to the anterior sacral ligament.

The surgeon performs additional dissection to ensure tension-free reduction of the neorectum. A 2-0 V-Lock stitch is placed in the serosa and sutured to the anterior sacral ligament for added support. The suture is tied, completing the neorectopexy. The repair is inspected, and hemostasis is achieved.
